mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate persistently above 5% raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of short-term fixed income, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 returns over the following quarter regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day returns is not negative and statistically significant (p>0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or average forward returns above 5% FEDFUNDS regime are not lower than below-5% regime.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between a high nominal federal funds rate and decelerating CPI inflation produces a rising real policy rate that tightens financial conditions faster than nominal rates alone suggest, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone captures this effect.
failure_condition: The joint real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY change) shows no significant negative correlation with forward 20-day returns beyond what FEDFUNDS alone explains (incremental Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declining housing starts reflect reduced interest-rate-sensitive demand and forewarn broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent outside tightening regimes.
failure_condition: HOUST decline fails to precede negative 60-day forward returns more often than a random baseline (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically within periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding real economic output and corporate revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in improved near-term earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO prints are not followed by above-median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales acceleration exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month once the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the effect is indistinguishable from unconditional base rate (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in payroll employment growth and a rising unemployment rate jointly indicate a genuine labor market inflection rather than noise, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; each series alone is too noisy to be reliably predictive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=156204.0 and UNRATE=3.6 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The joint signal (negative PAYEMS momentum combined with rising UNRATE) fails to produce Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or underperforms either single-series signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in real GDP growth signals approaching cyclical peak and reduced forward earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this relationship is expected to be muted or reversed during early-cycle recoveries.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: GDP deceleration episodes are not followed by below-median forward 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to late-cycle periods.
